purpose: Instructions for an LLM to generate a single MudProto asset payload JSON bundle that can be dropped into mudproto_server/configuration/assets/asset_payloads/ and loaded by the server.

response_contract.format: Return one raw JSON object only.
response_contract.delivery_requirement: The final result should be provided as a downloadable `.json` file, not just pasted as prose or wrapped in Markdown.
response_contract.schema_conformance_requirement: Every asset and the top-level payload must conform exactly to the provided schemas with no deviation, no extra fields, no renamed fields, no omitted required fields, and no unsupported structures.
forbidden: explanatory prose before or after the JSON
extra fields not present in the provided schemas
response_contract.filename_guidance: Use a short kebab-case filename such as sanctum-library-expansion.json
response_contract.interactive_behavior: If the user has not yet provided enough design direction, ask concise clarifying questions first and do not emit the final JSON payload until those answers are known.

pre_generation_discovery.rule: Before generating new content, the LLM should ask a short discovery set to anchor the zone into the existing world and confirm scope, tone, and mechanics.
required_questions: Which existing room or area should the new content attach to?
What should the new zone be about in terms of theme, story, or fantasy concept?
What kinds of content should it include, such as combat, merchants, lore, puzzles, boss fights, or support NPCs?
What difficulty band should the zone target?
Are there any special mechanics, gimmicks, hazards, or signature encounters to include?
How large should the zone be, approximately how many rooms?
Should the zone add new gear, items, spells, skills, or merchants?
pre_generation_discovery.output_behavior: If one or more of these details is missing, ask for them before generating the asset payload.

id_collision_prevention.rule: Append a GUID to every newly created asset ID and to payload_id to prevent collisions with existing or future content.
id_collision_prevention.override_exception: If the goal is to intentionally override an existing base-game asset, reuse the original asset ID exactly instead of appending a GUID.
id_collision_prevention.reference_rule: All cross-references inside the payload must use the same GUID-suffixed IDs, unless the payload is intentionally overriding an existing base-game asset by reusing its original ID.

conflict_resolution.policy: On asset ID conflict, the LLM payload should override the base-game definition.
conflict_resolution.rule: If the user wants to replace or modify an existing asset, reuse that asset's ID in the payload and provide the full replacement definition.
conflict_resolution.room_merge_behavior: If multiple asset payloads target the same room ID, merge the room exits across those payloads while keeping all other room fields from the last loaded room definition.
conflict_resolution.other_asset_behavior: For gear, items, spells, skills, NPCs, and zones, keep the last loaded asset definition when multiple payloads reuse the same ID.

payload_id.notes: Unique bundle identifier. Append a GUID suffix unless intentionally overriding existing content.

description.notes: Short human-readable summary of what the payload adds.

combat_and_abilities: Skills and spells support cast_type values self, target, and aoe (subject to asset-specific type constraints).
Damage and support abilities support rich player-facing context strings (damage_context, support_context, observer_action, observer_context).
Damage abilities support secondary restore effects via restore_effect/restore_ratio/restore_context fields.
Skills support cooldown controls including cooldown_rounds, cooldown_hours, and target_lag_rounds. lag_rounds is supported but currently only enforced for NPC entities, not players.
Support skills can scale over level steps using support_level_step and support_amount_per_level_step.

Support effects include heal, vigor, mana, damage_reduction, and extra_unarmed_hits on skills when explicitly desired. Skills and spells also support affect-based status behavior via affect_ids, using shared templates such as affect.received-damage, affect.dealt-damage, affect.regeneration, and affect.extra-hits. Affect entries may be plain ids or override objects with affect_id plus tuning fields.
Skills and spells support an element field (e.g. physical, storm, fire, restoration) used by element-aware affects such as affect.received-damage and affect.dealt-damage.
Skills support a target_posture field that forces the target into sitting on a successful hit. Sitting targets take increased damage and deal reduced damage.
Skills with usable_out_of_combat set to true can be used outside of combat for support effects.
Weapons support on-hit damage procs: room-wide (on_hit_room_damage_*) and single-target (on_hit_target_damage_*) with configurable chance, dice, and messages.
Casting a spell during combat skips the player's next melee attack round.
Spells support cooldown_rounds for NPC entity spell cooldowns; player spell cooldowns are not currently enforced.
npc_behavior_and_content: NPCs support aggro/ally/peaceful roles, merchant inventories and pricing, combat loadouts, and loot/inventory drops.
NPCs support conditional aggro via aggro_player_flags: players with matching flags are auto-engaged on entering the NPC's room.

NPCs support flag mutation on combat events: set_player_flags_on_hostile_action (when a player attacks the NPC), set_player_flags_on_death (on the killer), and set_world_flags_on_death (global). set_world_flags_on_death is critical for flag_spawns boss chains.
NPCs support corpse_label_style (generic or possessive) to control corpse naming independently of is_named.

NPCs support room_communications with audience control (private, room, both) plus player/world flag gating, flag mutation, and item-based gating (required_item_template_ids, excluded_item_template_ids).

NPCs support keyword_actions for interactive commands, including noop, grant_item, remove_item, award_experience, teleport_player, and exit reveal/hide actions. Keyword actions also support item-based gating.
NPCs support wandering via wander_chance and wander_room_ids (movement is constrained to directly connected exits that are also in wander_room_ids).

NPCs sharing the same wander_pack_id in the same room wander together as a pack. When any pack member triggers a wander, all same-room pack members with that pack ID move to the same destination. Engaged pack members are left behind.
Merchants support limited stock with quantity/base_quantity per inventory entry, and periodic restocking via merchant_restock_game_hours.
items_and_containers: Items support item_type values: consumable, potion, key, misc, and container.
Consumable and potion items support affect_ids to apply status effects on use, using the same affect system as skills and spells.
Key items have lock_ids matching lock_id values on exit_details or container items. Keys support consume_on_use to be destroyed after a successful unlock.
Container items support open/close/lock/unlock state, contents (default items spawned inside), coins, and portable (whether the container can be picked up).
Items support decay_game_hours for automatic expiry after a number of game hours, whether carried, dropped, or stored.
Item coin_value is the base monetary value used by merchant pricing with buy_markup and sell_ratio multipliers.
rooms_and_interactions: Rooms support seeded NPCs, seeded items, room_objects, keyword_actions, and exit_details metadata/state.
Exit details support close/open and lock/unlock state (including lock_id, can_lock, is_closed, is_locked, and optional message overrides).
Room exits support only north/south/east/west/up/down directions; diagonals are not supported.
Keyword actions support noop, grant_item, remove_item, award_experience, teleport_player, and room exit reveal/hide action types.
Keyword actions and room_communications support item-based gating via required_item_template_ids and excluded_item_template_ids.
Flavor-only interactions are supported through keyword_actions with noop-style messages and can be used for atmospheric commands like look shrine.
Room objects are inspectable objects that respond to look commands with keywords and descriptions.
Every room_objects entry must have a non-empty object_id, name, and description. The server rejects room_objects with blank or missing values for any of these fields.
zones_and_world_state: Zones support periodic repopulation via repopulate_game_hours.
Zones support repopulation reset controls: reset_player_flags, reset_world_flags, and reset_container_template_ids.
Zones support repopulation blockers via repopulation_blocking_item_template_ids and repopulation_block_cooldown_game_hours.
Zones support conditional NPC spawn orchestration through flag_spawns (required/excluded world flags, npc_id, room_id, count).
asset_payload_override_model: Payload entries can intentionally override base assets by reusing an existing asset ID.
Rooms merge exits across payloads with the last loaded room defining non-exit fields.
For non-room assets, last-loaded definition wins when IDs collide.

generation_rules: Keep all IDs unique within their asset type.
Append a GUID suffix to every new asset ID and payload_id unless you are intentionally overriding an existing base-game asset.
If an asset is meant to override base-game content, reuse the base asset ID exactly and treat the payload definition as the replacement.
If multiple asset payloads target the same room, their exits should accumulate while the most recently loaded room definition supplies the other room fields.
For other asset types with repeated IDs, keep the most recently loaded asset definition.
Use lowercase IDs and keywords to match project conventions.
When defining gear bonuses, use direct `equipment_effects` entries with only `effect_type` and `amount`.

For gear bonus effect_type values, use supported direct attribute ids such as `str`, `dex`, `con`, and `wis`, or configured shared gear stats such as `hit_points`, `vigor`, `mana`, `weapon_damage`, and `hitroll`.
Use equipment_effects for passive while-equipped bonuses, and reserve affect_ids for temporary support or combat status behavior from abilities or valid items.
Do not invent lore-only gear effect identifiers or unnecessary naming layers for equipment bonuses.

Before content creation, ask where the new content should attach, what the zone theme/content should be, the target difficulty, any special mechanics, and the desired room count unless the user already supplied that information.
Every asset section must be present and must be a JSON array, even when empty.
Deliver the final output as a downloadable `.json` file suitable for saving into `mudproto_server/configuration/assets/asset_payloads/`.
Conform every asset and the top-level payload exactly to the provided schemas with no deviation.
Never leave any ID field (template_id, spell_id, skill_id, npc_id, room_id, zone_id, object_id) as an empty string. Every ID must be a meaningful, non-blank value.
Never leave name or description fields as empty strings on any asset entry. Every name and description must contain meaningful text.
Only use fields supported by the schemas below.
Do not invent, rename, reorder semantically, or omit schema-defined structure beyond what the schemas explicitly allow.

Some fields are only appropriate for certain NPC types; for example merchant and selling-related properties should only appear on merchant-style NPCs, and peaceful flags should only be used when the NPC concept actually calls for them.
Only reference assets that exist in the base game or in this same payload.

Do not create new consumable items, spells, or skills that are only cosmetic or lore reskins of existing mechanics. If the gameplay is materially the same, reuse or intentionally override the existing asset instead of adding a near-duplicate.
You may create fully new spells and skills when they are flavorful, mechanically appropriate, and strongly matched to the theme, lore, and tone of the zone and the broader MudProto world.
You may borrow from, reuse, extend, or reference existing game assets when it helps the design, but you do not have to; you may also create fully new assets when appropriate.
Preserve MudProto's existing fantasy tone and naming style.
Room descriptions should be 3-4 sentences long and should clearly reinforce the atmosphere, story, and theme of the zone they belong to.

All room interconnections must be logical in-world and spatially coherent. Avoid random or immersion-breaking adjacency, and make connected rooms feel like they belong next to each other in a believable layout.
If a final boss or locked area is gated behind multiple prerequisite kills or events, make sure every prerequisite emits the exact world flag the gate checks.
Do not leave bypass routes open for gated progression. If a keep, vault, or sanctum is meant to unlock later, omit or hide that exit from the room's default exits until the gated action reveals it.
Do not place a gated final boss in the room's default npc list. Prefer a zone flag_spawns rule with required_world_flags so the boss appears only after the prerequisites are met.
Whenever appropriate, add at least one optional flavor interaction that is not required for progression or combat, such as a keyword action like 'look shrine' that returns atmospheric text.
Flavor interactions should enrich worldbuilding and roleplay even when they have no mechanical reward.

Be maximally creative with room names, spell names, skill names, item flavor, lore hooks, atmospheric details, and worldbuilding flair as long as every asset remains fully compliant with the provided schemas.
Prefer small, coherent bundles that describe one area, quest pocket, encounter set, merchant restock, or feature addition.
Room exits may only use the directions north, south, east, west, up, and down. Diagonal directions such as northeast, northwest, southeast, and southwest are not supported.
damage_context and support_context strings that end with '!' or '?' must not also include a trailing period. The server automatically handles terminal punctuation for '.', '!', and '?'.

For damage_context and support_context, write perspective-safe text that works for both player recipients and named NPC recipients. Prefer placeholders like [a/an] and [verb] rather than fixed second-person possessives.

Do not use actor-POV lines like 'You drive a knife into your foe!' inside damage_context/support_context. Use neutral target-state wording such as '[a/an] [verb] thrown off balance by the strike!' so rendering remains grammatically correct for all targets.
Set is_named explicitly on generated NPCs: true for unique proper-name or story-significant characters, false for ordinary generic mobs.
When is_named is true, the NPC corpse label should read as the full-name possessive form, such as 'Brother Cleft's corpse'.

Skills with target_lag_rounds > 0 inflict command lag on the target when hit. The attacking entity also self-lags for the same number of rounds, blocking its special abilities but not its melee attacks.

NPCs with wander_chance > 0 and a non-empty wander_room_ids list will periodically move between rooms. All rooms in wander_room_ids should form a connected subgraph via their exits so the NPC can actually traverse between them. The NPC will only move to rooms that are both in wander_room_ids AND directly reachable via its current room's exits.
NPCs that are currently engaged in combat with any player will not wander.

To make NPCs wander as a pack, give them the same wander_pack_id value. All pack members should share the same wander_room_ids so they can travel the same routes. Pack members engaged in combat are left behind when the rest of the pack moves.

Zones support a flag_spawns array for conditional NPC spawns triggered by world flag state. Each entry spawns an NPC into a specific room immediately after any NPC death that sets world flags, provided the NPC (by npc_id) is not already alive in the target room. Use this for scripted encounter progressions such as spawning a boss when a mini boss dies.

flag_spawns entries fire every time the flag conditions are met and no matching NPC is alive in the room. To ensure a boss spawns exactly once per cycle, use reset_world_flags on repopulation to clear the trigger flags, which prevents the boss from re-spawning until the zone resets.
When is_named is true, also set corpse_label_style to possessive so the corpse renders as the NPC's possessive name form.
NPCs that should set world flags on death must include set_world_flags_on_death in the NPC template. This is the mechanism that triggers flag_spawns rules.

When designing faction or reputation NPCs, use aggro_player_flags to make NPCs auto-attack players who have been flagged by hostile actions against their faction, and set_player_flags_on_hostile_action to apply the flag when a player attacks.
Key items should have lock_ids that match the lock_id on the corresponding exit_details or container item they are meant to unlock.
Container items placed in rooms should generally set portable to false for large environmental containers like chests.
When creating quest items or keys that should not persist indefinitely, set decay_game_hours or consume_on_use as appropriate.

Keyword action types grant_item, remove_item, and award_experience each have specific required fields: grant_item needs template_id (and optional quantity, if_missing), remove_item needs template_id (and optional quantity), and award_experience needs amount.
Consumable and potion items can apply status effects via affect_ids using the same affect system as skills and spells.
data_integrity: Every asset entry must have a non-empty primary ID (template_id, spell_id, skill_id, npc_id, room_id, zone_id, or object_id for room_objects). The server rejects any asset with a blank or missing ID.
Every asset entry that has a name field must provide a non-empty name string. The server rejects assets with blank or missing names.
Every room_objects entry must include non-empty object_id, name, and description fields.
Room object names are automatically title-cased by the server on load, so payloads should use lowercase or title case names.
cross_reference_requirements: rooms[].zone_id must match a zone_id in the base assets or this payload.
rooms[].npcs[].npc_id must match an NPC in the base assets or this payload.
rooms[].exits[direction] must point to a valid room_id.
rooms[].items[].template_id must match an item template_id in the base assets or this payload.
npcs[].main_hand_weapon.template_id and npcs[].off_hand_weapon.template_id must match gear template_id values.
npcs[].inventory_items[].template_id and npcs[].merchant_inventory[].template_id must match a gear or item template_id.
spells[].damage_scaling_attribute_id and skills[].scaling_attribute_id should use valid attribute ids such as str, dex, con, int, or wis.
npcs[].wander_room_ids[] must match valid room_id values in the base assets or this payload.
zones[].flag_spawns[].npc_id must match an NPC template in the base assets or this payload.
zones[].flag_spawns[].room_id must match a room that belongs to this zone.
npcs[].keyword_actions[].required_item_template_ids[] and excluded_item_template_ids[] must match item template_id values.
npcs[].room_communications[].required_item_template_ids[] and excluded_item_template_ids[] must match item template_id values.
rooms[].keyword_actions[].required_item_template_ids[] and excluded_item_template_ids[] must match item template_id values.
items[].lock_ids[] on key items must match lock_id values on exit_details or container items.
items[].affect_ids[] on consumable/potion items must match valid affect template ids.
authoring_order: Create gear, items, spells, and skills first.
Create NPCs that reference those assets.
Create rooms that place those NPCs.
Create or update zones if needed.
Validate all cross-references before returning the final JSON.
minimum_quality_bar: Descriptions should be concise, usable in-game, and support the intended fantasy atmosphere.
Room descriptions should be 3-4 sentences in length and should tie directly into the theme of their zone.
Room interconnections should feel natural and navigationally believable, with adjacency that makes sense for the fiction and layout of the area.
Room titles, spell names, skill names, and NPC names should feel vivid, flavorful, and memorable while fitting MudProto's tone.
Combat/support context strings should be ready for player-facing text.
Combat/support context strings should be perspective-safe for both 'you' and named targets, avoiding recipient-relative nouns like 'your foe'.
Include optional flavor-only interaction hooks (for example room or NPC keyword actions with noop-style output text) when they naturally fit the scene.
Lore, atmosphere, and thematic flair are strongly encouraged whenever they fit within the schema-defined fields.
New spells and skills should feel vivid, flavorful, and lore-native to the zone or encounter set they belong to rather than generic filler abilities.
Merchant inventories should be sensible for the NPC role.
Avoid redundant consumables, spells, and skills that duplicate existing mechanics under a new name unless the user explicitly requests a distinct variant or override.
Gear bonus authoring should also stay broad and reusable: prefer direct stat bonuses over bespoke one-off effect labels.
Boss-gated zones should be progression-safe: no always-open bypass to the finale, no missing prerequisite flags, and no pre-spawned final boss when the design calls for an unlock.
Avoid overpowered numbers unless explicitly requested.
Before returning the final JSON, verify that every asset has a non-empty primary ID and non-empty name. This is the most common cause of server-side validation failures.

persistent.notes: When false, the gear item is purged from character inventory/equipment on permanent disconnect or logout-style cleanup flows.

weapon_type.notes: Weapon-only field. Allowed values come from `configuration/attributes/weapon_types.json`.

can_two_hand.notes: Weapon-only field. Allows a main-hand weapon to be used with both hands when needed.

requires_two_hands.notes: Weapon-only field. When true, the weapon must occupy both hands.

on_hit_room_damage_chance.notes: Weapon-only field. Percent or fractional chance for an on-hit room-wide damage proc; `33` and `0.33` both mean 33%.

on_hit_room_damage_dice_count.notes: Weapon-only field. Damage dice count for the room proc.

on_hit_room_damage_dice_sides.notes: Weapon-only field. Damage dice sides for the room proc.

on_hit_room_damage_roll_modifier.notes: Weapon-only flat modifier added to the room proc damage roll.

on_hit_room_damage_message.notes: Private message shown to the wielder when the proc fires. Supports `[weapon_name]`.

on_hit_room_damage_observer_message.notes: Broadcast message shown to other players in the room when the proc fires. Supports `[actor_name]` and `[weapon_name]`.

on_hit_target_damage_chance.notes: Weapon-only field. Percent or fractional chance for an additional single-target on-hit damage proc against the engaged enemy; `33` and `0.33` both mean 33%.

on_hit_target_damage_dice_count.notes: Weapon-only field. Damage dice count for the single-target proc.

on_hit_target_damage_dice_sides.notes: Weapon-only field. Damage dice sides for the single-target proc.

on_hit_target_damage_roll_modifier.notes: Weapon-only flat modifier added to the single-target proc damage roll.

on_hit_target_damage_message.notes: Private message shown to the wielder when the single-target proc fires. Supports `[weapon_name]`.

on_hit_target_damage_observer_message.notes: Broadcast message shown to other players in the room when the single-target proc fires. Supports `[actor_name]` and `[weapon_name]`.

equipment_effects.notes: Optional direct passive gear bonuses applied while equipped. Each entry should include only `effect_type` and `amount`. `effect_type` can be any configured attribute id such as `str`, `dex`, `con`, `wis`, or a general stat type from configuration/attributes/equipment_effects.json such as `hit_points`, `vigor`, `mana`, `weapon_damage`, or `hitroll`. Use this for worn stat bonuses rather than temporary affect-style behavior.

wear_slots.notes: Required for armor, must be empty for weapons.

item_type.notes: Use potion directly for potion items instead of a separate subtype field.

effect_type.notes: Required for consumable restore items; omit for keys and misc items.

effect_target.notes: Required for consumable restore items; omit for keys and misc items.

effect_amount.notes: Required for consumable restore items; omit for keys and misc items.

persistent.notes: When false, the item is purged from character inventory/equipment on permanent disconnect or future logout-style cleanup flows.

lock_ids.notes: For keys, list the lock IDs this item can lock or unlock.

portable.notes: For container items, when false the container can exist in the room but cannot be picked up into inventory. The alias `carryable` is also accepted.

contents.notes: For containers, default item contents that are spawned inside when the container is created or reset from its template.

template_id.notes: Must match an existing gear or item template id.

consume_on_use.notes: For keys or other special items, when true the item is removed after a successful use such as unlocking a chest.

consume_message.notes: Optional text shown when a consume_on_use item is spent.

decay_game_hours.notes: When greater than zero, the item automatically decays after this many game hours whether carried, dropped, or stored in a container.

decay_message.notes: Optional text for decay-driven expiry; currently most useful for carried items.

can_close.notes: For containers, controls whether `open` and `close` commands apply.

can_lock.notes: For containers, controls whether `lock` and `unlock` commands apply.

lock_id.notes: For lockable containers, the single lock id that a matching key item can open.

coin_value.notes: Base monetary value used by merchant pricing. Merchants apply their buy_markup and sell_ratio multipliers to this value.

coins.notes: For containers, initial coin contents spawned inside when the container is created or reset.

affect_ids.notes: For consumables and potions, affect references applied to the user on use. Entries may be strings or objects with affect_id plus override fields. Uses the same affect system as skills and spells.

is_named.notes: Explicit naming control. Set true for uniquely named or story-significant NPCs that should render without an indefinite article in combat and room text; leave false for ordinary generic mobs. Named NPC corpses render as full possessive labels such as 'Brother Cleft's corpse'.

aggro_player_flags.notes: When a player with any of these player flags enters the NPC's room, the NPC auto-engages them in combat.

set_player_flags_on_hostile_action.notes: Player flags set on the attacking player when they initiate combat against this NPC.

set_player_flags_on_death.notes: Player flags set on the killing player when this NPC dies.

set_world_flags_on_death.notes: World flags set when this NPC dies. Critical for flag_spawns chains where killing one NPC triggers spawning of the next.

corpse_label_style.notes: Controls corpse naming. generic produces 'the corpse of a Goblin', possessive produces 'Brother Cleft's corpse'. Typically use possessive for is_named NPCs.

merchant_restock_game_hours.notes: If greater than 0, limited merchant stock replenishes to its configured quantity after this many game hours.

template_id.notes: Must match an existing gear template_id when provided.

drop_on_death.notes: Percent chance for this equipped weapon to appear on the corpse when the NPC dies.

template_id.notes: Must match an existing gear template_id when provided.

drop_on_death.notes: Percent chance for this equipped weapon to appear on the corpse when the NPC dies.

audience.notes: `private` shows only to the entering player, `room` broadcasts only to the room, and `both` does both.

message.notes: Shown when the configured trigger fires, such as a player entering the NPC's room.

required_item_template_ids.notes: Player must have all of these items in inventory or equipment for this communication to fire.

excluded_item_template_ids.notes: Communication is suppressed if the player has any of these items.

keyword_actions.notes: NPC-side command triggers. Uses the same structure as room keyword_actions and supports noop, grant_item, remove_item, award_experience, teleport_player, and room exit reveal/hide actions.

actions.notes: Supports noop, grant_item, remove_item, award_experience, teleport_player, and the standard room exit reveal/hide action types.

required_item_template_ids.notes: Player must have all of these items in inventory or equipment for this action to be available.

excluded_item_template_ids.notes: Action is unavailable if the player has any of these items.

template_id.notes: Must match an existing gear or item template id. Spawned items transfer to the corpse on NPC death.

spawn_chance.notes: Percent chance for each quantity instance to appear on the NPC when it spawns.

wander_chance.notes: Probability each tick that this NPC attempts to wander. 0.0 disables wandering. Typical range is 0.05-0.15.

wander_room_ids.notes: Room IDs this NPC is allowed to wander between. The NPC will only move to rooms that are both in this list AND directly reachable via its current room's exits. All listed rooms should form a connected subgraph.

wander_pack_id.notes: When set, all NPCs sharing the same wander_pack_id in the same room move together as a pack when any one of them triggers a wander. Leave empty for independent wandering.

exits.notes: Only cardinal directions (north, south, east, west) and up/down are supported. Diagonal directions are not allowed.

items.description: Optional ground items seeded into the room when the world or zone initializes.

template_id.notes: Must match an existing gear or item template id.

keyword_actions.description: Room-side command triggers. Supports optional debug_only boolean to include an action only when gameplay.debug_mode is enabled in server settings.

required_item_template_ids.notes: Player must have all of these items in inventory or equipment for this action to be available.

excluded_item_template_ids.notes: Action is unavailable if the player has any of these items.

actions.notes: Supports noop, grant_item, remove_item, award_experience, teleport_player, and room exit reveal/hide action types.

debug_only.notes: When true, this keyword action is only available when gameplay.debug_mode is enabled in server settings.

room_objects.description: Inspectable objects in the room that respond to look commands.

keywords.notes: Words the player can use to look at this object, e.g. ['shrine', 'altar'].

description.notes: Text shown when the player looks at this object.

debug_only.notes: When true, this room object is only present when gameplay.debug_mode is enabled in server settings.

exit_details.description: Optional exit metadata/state. Supports close/open and lock/unlock behavior. Grammar defaults are generated from the exit name/type/direction when omitted.

name.notes: Display name for the exit, e.g. 'iron gate', 'wooden door'.

exit_type.notes: Describes the type of exit for grammar purposes, e.g. 'door', 'gate', 'hatch'.

keywords.notes: Words the player can use to target this exit with open/close/lock/unlock commands.

lock_id.notes: The lock id that a matching key item can lock or unlock.

element.notes: Damage element for this skill (e.g. physical, storm, fire, restoration). Used by element-aware affects such as damage_received_multiplier.

cast_type.default: target for damage, self for support

support_effect.required: for support skills (may be empty string for affect-only support skills)
support_effect.notes: Use empty string when the skill is affect-only (all behavior defined by affect_ids). extra_unarmed_hits requires support_level_step > 0.

affect_ids.notes: Array of affect references defined in configuration/attributes/affects.json. Entries may be strings or objects with affect_id plus override fields. Common templates: affect.received-damage, affect.dealt-damage, affect.regeneration, affect.extra-hits.

duration_rounds.notes: Use for round-based support skills such as temporary damage reduction stances.

lag_rounds.notes: Lag rounds applied after using the skill. Currently only applies to NPC entities; player skill lag is not enforced.

cooldown_hours.notes: Game-hour-based cooldown. When set, the skill cannot be used again until the specified number of in-game hours have passed.

target_lag_rounds.notes: Number of combat rounds of command lag inflicted on the target when hit. The attacking entity also self-lags for the same number of rounds, blocking its own special abilities but not its melee attacks.

support_level_step.notes: Every this many character levels, support_amount_per_level_step is added to the support effect. Only relevant for support skills.

support_amount_per_level_step.notes: Bonus support amount granted per support_level_step interval of character levels.

target_posture.notes: When set to sitting, forces the target into sitting posture on a successful hit. Sitting targets take increased damage and deal reduced damage.

element.notes: Damage element for this spell (e.g. storm, fire, restoration, blood). Used by element-aware affects such as damage_received_multiplier.

cast_type.default: target for damage, self for support

support_effect.required: for support spells (may be empty string for affect-only support spells)
support_effect.notes: Use empty string when the spell is affect-only (all behavior defined by affect_ids).

affect_ids.notes: Array of affect references defined in configuration/attributes/affects.json. Entries may be strings or objects with affect_id plus override fields. Common templates: affect.received-damage, affect.dealt-damage, affect.regeneration, affect.extra-hits.

cooldown_rounds.notes: Combat-round-based cooldown for NPC spell use. When set, NPC entities cannot reuse this spell until this many rounds have passed. Player spell cooldowns are not currently enforced.

reset_player_flags.notes: When the zone repopulates, these per-player interaction flags are cleared for all active characters.

reset_world_flags.notes: When the zone repopulates, these shared world flags are cleared for the live world state.

reset_container_template_ids.notes: When the zone repopulates, any room containers using these item template ids are reset to their configured default state and contents.

repopulation_blocking_item_template_ids.notes: If any of these item templates exist anywhere in the live world or a connected character inventory, the zone's repopulation remains blocked.

repopulation_block_cooldown_game_hours.notes: After the blocking items disappear, the zone stays blocked for this many additional game hours before repopulation can resume.

flag_spawns.notes: Conditional NPC spawns triggered by world flag state. Each entry spawns NPCs into a room when the specified world flags are set (and excluded flags are absent) and no matching NPC is already alive there. Evaluated immediately after any NPC death that sets world flags.

npc_id.notes: The NPC template ID to spawn.

room_id.notes: The room to spawn the NPC into. Must be a room belonging to this zone.

count.notes: How many instances of the NPC to spawn.

required_world_flags.notes: All of these world flags must be set for the spawn to trigger.

excluded_world_flags.notes: If any of these world flags are set, the spawn is suppressed.

announcement_message.notes: Optional zone-wide announcement sent to connected players in the zone when this flag-spawn rule actually fires. Useful for boss-unlock or escalation messaging.

gear.name: Training Sword
gear.description: A blunted practice sword issued to new recruits.

gear.name: Training Dagger
gear.description: A lightweight practice dagger for off-hand drills.

gear.name: Scout Dagger
gear.description: A worn dagger taken from a hall scout.

gear.name: Prototype Dawnblade
gear.description: A keen sunsteel sword with a balanced grip and a pale edge that seems to hold the first light of morning.

gear.name: Reaver Nightblade
gear.description: A blackened longsword etched with pale runes that drink the brazier light.

gear.name: Exarch Doomblade
gear.description: A massive rune-scarred sword that hums with crushing arcane pressure.

gear.name: Exarch Emberfang
gear.description: A hooked offhand blade glowing faintly along its ember-bright edge.

gear.name: Sunscour
gear.description: A radiant war axe of pale gold steel whose edge holds a captive dawn; every clean strike threatens to spill sunlight across the whole battlefield.

gear.on_hit_room_damage_observer_message: [actor_name]'s [weapon_name] erupts with sacred sunlight, scorching every enemy in the room!

gear.name: Daystar
gear.description: A dense offhand cudgel banded in sun-bright bronze; each sharp strike can flare into a focused burst of punishing light.

gear.on_hit_target_damage_observer_message: [actor_name]'s [weapon_name] cracks with focused sunlight and batters the target again!

gear.name: Vanguard Jacket
gear.description: A reinforced jacket worn by front-line trainees.

gear.name: Vanguard Boots
gear.description: Heavy boots built for marching and impact.

gear.name: Arcanist Robe
gear.description: A rune-lined robe favored by novice arcanists.

gear.name: Arcanist Wraps
gear.description: Light hand wraps that steady precise gestures.

gear.name: Amber Ring
gear.description: A simple brass ring used for slot testing.

gear.name: Azure Ring
gear.description: A polished silver ring used for multi-slot testing.

gear.name: Practice Vest
gear.description: A padded vest used to test occupied chest-slot behavior.

gear.name: Practice Cloak
gear.description: A padded cloak used to test occupied back-slot behavior.

gear.name: Roadstrider Boots
gear.description: Supple dark boots reinforced with ridgehide and weighted for perfect balance over loose roadstone.

gear.name: Stoneheart Cloak
gear.description: A heavy mantle lined with kiln-dried plates that steady the breath and harden the body against fatigue.

gear.name: Waywarden Bracers
gear.description: Lacquered travel bracers etched with mile-marks that guide every strike into the surest opening.

gear.name: Ridgebreaker Harness
gear.description: A broad leather-and-iron harness built for caravan breakers who fought like battering rams.

gear.name: Twinway Signet
gear.description: A roadwarden signet cut with two crossing trails, said to favor both quick feet and stubborn lungs.

gear.name: Wicked Curved Scimitar
gear.description: A wickedly curved scimitar, its edge notched from hard use on the road.

items.name: Potion of Mana
items.description: A glass vial of bright blue liquid that restores magical focus.

items.name: Potion of Mending
items.description: A small crimson draught that closes bruises and shallow cuts.

items.name: Potion of Vigor
items.description: A lively golden tonic that steadies the breath and restores battle stamina.

items.name: Supply Chest
items.description: A banded supply chest sits open against the wall, stocked with potions, loose coin, and a prize blade for a promising newcomer.

items.name: Brass Gate Key
items.description: A small brass key with a worn square bit, likely meant for the western gate nearby.

items.name: Loft Chest Key
items.description: A bronze key etched with a simple sunburst, evidently meant for a locked chest tucked away above the starting chamber.
items.consume_message: The Loft Chest Key crumbles to dust after turning in the lock.
items.decay_message: The Loft Chest Key crumbles to dust as Aurek's fading blessing leaves it.

items.name: Weathered Chest
items.description: A weathered cedar chest bound in dark iron bands rests beneath the loft rafters, sturdy and too awkward to carry off whole.

rooms.title: Prototype Chamber
rooms.description: A plain stone chamber used for early server testing. A stout western gate bars a road beyond, while scuff marks and a worn pull-ring on the sealed iron hatch overhead suggest it can be worked open by hand.

keyword_actions.message: You pull the iron hatch open. A ladder now leads up.
You pull the hatch shut, hiding the ladder above.
The magic circle flares beneath your feet and whisks you away in a breath of pale light.

You press the easy button. A cheerful chime rings out.
You gain 1000 experience.

room_objects.name: iron hatch
room_objects.description: A square iron hatch is set into the ceiling overhead. Its worn pull-ring and the scrape marks around the frame suggest it can be worked open or shut by hand.

room_objects.name: west gate
room_objects.description: A stout iron gate bars the western opening. Its latch looks simple enough to work by hand if it has not been locked.

room_objects.name: magic circle
room_objects.description: A faintly glowing circle of chalk and old runes is scribed into the stone floor. The lines pulse softly, as if waiting for someone to stand within them.

room_objects.name: easy button
room_objects.description: A large candy-red button mounted on a squat pedestal. The word EASY is stamped across its face in cheerful white letters.

rooms.title: West Road
rooms.description: A weathered road of packed earth and broken stone stretches westward past the gate, quiet beneath a pale open sky.

room_objects.name: east gate
room_objects.description: A stout iron gate stands in the eastern opening back toward the chamber. Its latch looks simple enough to work by hand if it has not been locked.

rooms.title: West Road — Southward Bend
rooms.description: The packed-earth road bends sharply south here where a mossy waystone heeled over long ago. Deep cart ruts cut through the verge on either side.

rooms.title: West Road — Stone Defile
rooms.description: The road squeezes through a narrow cut between two weathered stone banks, overhung by tangles of briar and twisted root. The footing is treacherous where loose scree has spilled across the path.

rooms.title: West Road — Muddy Ford
rooms.description: A shallow brook cuts straight across the road here, its stepping stones slick with algae and mud churned up by travellers. The road resumes on the far bank, bending north.

rooms.title: West Road — Ridgeline Climb
rooms.description: The road climbs steeply here as it winds up a low ridge, loose stones clattering underfoot beneath a grey and overcast sky. A broken dry-stone wall flanks the uphill side.

rooms.title: West Road — Old Clearing
rooms.description: The road opens onto a wide, overgrown clearing where old milestone markers lean at angles in the weedy verge. A cold wind moves through the long grass and there is nothing but empty country beyond.

rooms.title: Hidden Loft
rooms.description: Dusty rafters and a narrow service ledge overlook the prototype chamber below.

rooms.title: South Market
rooms.description: A sheltered trade nook of canvas awnings and tidy crates opens here, warm with lamplight and the scent of oil and leather.

rooms.title: Northern Hall
rooms.description: A narrow hall of cold stone extends here, quiet and still.

rooms.title: Eastern Watch
rooms.description: A long watch corridor lit by guttering iron braziers and scarred by old blade marks.

rooms.title: Whispering Sanctum
rooms.description: Rune-etched stone and drifting embers hum with unstable arcane current.

rooms.title: Ashen Reliquary
rooms.description: Obsidian pillars and old cinder braziers crowd this sunken vault, where warded steel hums like a storm about to break.

skills.observer_action: [actor_name] snaps out a quick jab.

skills.observer_context: Measured breaths steady [actor_possessive] posture and knit shallow wounds.

skills.support_context: You settle into a centered guard, turning the worst of each blow aside.
skills.observer_action: [actor_name] plants [actor_possessive] feet and draws a steady breath.
skills.observer_context: [actor_name] settles into a centered guard that blunts incoming strikes.

skills.name: Bash

skills.damage_context: [a/an] [verb] jolted as a precise pressure point is struck.
skills.observer_action: [actor_name] snaps a precise strike at a vulnerable nerve cluster.

skills.name: Roundhouse Kick

skills.damage_context: [a/an] [verb] battered by a whirling roundhouse kick.

skills.name: Fist Flurry

skills.support_context: Your fists blur into a relentless flurry.
skills.observer_action: [actor_name] erupts into a blinding flurry of strikes.

skills.description: You dive into the press of battle and drag a companion clear of the worst blow.
skills.support_context: You hurl yourself into danger and draw the enemy's fury away.

spells.name: Spark
spells.description: A focused bolt of crackling force.

spells.name: Ember Lance
spells.description: A fan of burning embers that scorches nearby enemies.

spells.name: Ice Storm
spells.description: A roaring burst of sleet and jagged ice that lashes every hostile foe nearby.
spells.damage_context: [a/an] [verb] raked by freezing shards and numbing wind.

spells.name: Healing Light
spells.description: A sudden restorative pulse that immediately heals your wounds.
spells.support_context: A sudden wave of restorative light surges through you, mending injuries at once.

spells.name: Regeneration Ward
spells.description: A steady restorative ward that heals you each battle round, even outside combat.
spells.support_context: A pale ward settles around you, knitting your wounds with each heartbeat of battle.
spells.observer_action: [actor_name] focuses deeply, weaving a regeneration ward.
spells.observer_context: A pale ward settles around [actor_object], knitting wounds with each heartbeat of battle.

spells.name: Arc Bolt
spells.description: A rigid line of blue force cracks outward.

spells.name: Life Siphon
spells.description: Threads of crimson light loop back to the caster.
spells.restore_context: Crimson vitality floods back into you.

content.description: Adds a hidden tomb beneath the Sunscour Redoubt vault, including a boss that drops the sepulcher key sought by the paladins.

gear.name: Censer Chain

gear.name: Sepulcher Maul
gear.description: A square-headed war maul stained with old soot and prayer-oil.

gear.name: Funerary Plate
gear.description: Laminar plates lacquered in ash-black varnish and inscribed with dim protective verses.

items.name: Sepulcher Key

items.name: charred prayer beads

spells.name: Graveflare

spells.damage_context: [a/an] [verb] scorched by a hush of ash and funeral flame.

spells.observer_context: A stern pall of ash gathers over [actor_object] and some of the hurt closes.

skills.damage_context: [a/an] [verb] hooked hard by a splintering rake of bone and iron.
skills.observer_action: [actor_name] lunges in with a jagged, dragging strike.
